commit.message:
Add AWS and Azure Managed Grafana plugins (#7)

Add two new SRE Agent plugins:

- aws: AWS integration providing secure access to AWS services including
  documentation, API calls, and operational procedures via the AWS MCP Server.

- azure-managed-grafana: Azure Managed Grafana integration providing access to
  dashboards, Azure Monitor metrics, Application Insights, Azure Data Explorer,
  SQL Server, and Resource Graph queries via the built-in AMG-MCP endpoint.

Both plugins include plugin.json, .mcp.json, README.md, and SKILL.md.
marketplace.json updated with both entries.

Co-authored-by: Dheeraj Bandaru <dbandaru@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix socket hang up on C4 API calls with Node 22+ keep-alive

Node.js 19+ defaults the global HTTPS agent to keepAlive: true, but the
Control4 cloud API sends Connection: close. Back-to-back requests (login
then controller list) could reuse a stale socket, causing 'socket hang up'
errors and a 500 on the controllers endpoint.

- Add agent option pass-through in requestText (http-client.js)
- Create a dedicated https.Agent({ keepAlive: false }) for C4 cloud calls
- Apply c4Agent to all four C4 API request sites in server.js

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Initial release: Azure SRE Agent Plugin Creator skill

A coding-agent skill that guides AI assistants through creating Azure SRE Agent
plugins. Supports GitHub Copilot CLI, Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, and OpenCode.

Includes:
- skills/sre-plugin-creator/SKILL.md — the core skill
- examples/ — complete AWS and Azure Managed Grafana plugin examples
- docs/ — platform-specific installation guides
- Platform configs for .copilot, .opencode, .codex

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix TTL rounding to match Redis behavior (#1602)

* Fix TTL rounding to match Redis behavior (#1389)

Redis rounds TTL seconds to the nearest integer (equivalent to (ms+500)/1000),
while Garnet was truncating (flooring) via a direct (long) cast. This caused
SET+EXPIRE 10+TTL to return 9 instead of 10 when called immediately.

Fix: Use Math.Round(MidpointRounding.AwayFromZero) in both
SecondsFromDiffUtcNowTicks and MillisecondsFromDiffUtcNowTicks in
ConvertUtils.cs.

Added TtlRoundingTest to verify string and object keys return the expected
TTL value immediately after EXPIRE.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* increase test timing tolerance

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Improve telemetry error classification with typed sentinels (#7051)

* Eliminate opaque errors_errorString from telemetry

Looked at error telemetry for azd 1.23.7 and 1.23.8. About
15k were just 'internal.errors_errorString' and ~3.6k were
'error.suggestion' — not useful for debugging.

Why this was happening:

- MapError was matching ErrorWithSuggestion wrapper before
  looking at the real error inside, so things like auth and
  ARM failures got misclassified.
- Most Run() methods used bare errors.New or fmt.Errorf with
  no typed error, so MapError had nothing to match on and
  dumped them in the catch-all.

What this does:

- Unwrap ErrorWithSuggestion first so the real error gets
  classified
- Add 28 typed errors covering all commands
- Fix all 54 bare error sites to wrap typed errors with %w
- Add ErrorWithSuggestion at each site for better agent/CLI
  experience
- Add an AST test that blocks new bare errors from being added

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove non-actionable suggestions in extensions.go

Return base errors instead of wrapping with unhelpful
'This is an internal error...' suggestion text.

Addresses JeffreyCA review feedback.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert ErrorWithSuggestion unwrap logic

Restore original error.suggestion + error.type classification.
Wei confirmed the empty error.type was a backend GDPR
classification issue, now fixed. The original design is correct:
ErrorWithSuggestion produces error.suggestion as ResultCode
with the inner error type in the error.type attribute.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove dead sentinel checks and classify via ErrorWithSuggestion

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add cross-conversation AI memory system

Add a ChatGPT-style memory system where the AI automatically remembers
facts and preferences about users across separate conversations.

New features:
- user_memories and user_preferences database tables
- save_memory / delete_memory AI tools (LLM decides when to save)
- Memories injected into system prompt for each request
- Per-user toggle (default: on) via GET/PATCH /preferences
- Memory CRUD via GET/DELETE /memories endpoints
- All memory content encrypted per-user (AES-256-GCM)

fix: resolve static analysis bugs across src/

- Fix switch subject injection in Case/LogicStep (subject/conditional out of sync)
- Fix duplicate result push in Workflow (step() and execute() both pushed)
- Fix failed steps incorrectly reported as success in Workflow/Step
- Fix should_break written to global State and never reset
- Fix State.each() not awaiting async callbacks (forEach → for...of)
- Fix DelayStep/SwitchStep passing `type` instead of `step_type` to super()
- Fix missing comparator keys in conditional_step_comparators enum
- Fix console.error never triggered (endsWith case mismatch in base.js)
- Fix SwitchStep calling .bind() on non-function default_callable
- Fix Case.switch_subject setter rejecting valid falsy subjects
- Fix LogicStep passing undefined base_type (step_types.LOGIC_STEP)
- Remove unimplemented CRON type from delay_types enum
- Add workflow_break_executed to workflow_event_names enum

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(merge_queue): set worker thread daemon=True to prevent process hang on ungraceful shutdown

The worker thread was created with daemon=False, which meant if the process
exited ungracefully (unhandled exception, uncaught KeyboardInterrupt), the
non-daemon thread would keep the process alive indefinitely waiting for the
queue to drain. Setting daemon=True allows the process to exit cleanly in
those cases while still relying on the explicit shutdown path
(request_shutdown -> merge_queue.shutdown) for graceful drain.

Cherry-picked from task/PokePoke-jjxz.
Closes: PokePoke-jjxz

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: stop toAppError regex from masking Prisma errors as 404

The /not found/i regex in toAppError() caught Prisma infrastructure
errors (e.g. 'table not found') and converted them to HTTP 404,
hiding the real error (missing DB, unmigrated tables). This caused
the /api/workspace 404 on Vercel.

- Remove overly broad 'not found' regex from toAppError
- Use explicit AppError(404) in getCurrentWorkspace instead
- Update test mocks to throw AppError matching real service behavior

Add BFloat16 support via Core.BFloat16 tangent infrastructure and BFloat16s extension (#1042)

* Add Core.BFloat16 tangent infrastructure and rules

- Tangent space of BFloat16 is BFloat16 itself (mirrors IEEEFloat treatment)
- Implements all tangent/fdata/rdata infrastructure methods
- Conversion rules: BFloat16 <-> Float32/Float64
- Math rules: sqrt, cbrt, exp*, log*, trig, hyperbolic, hypot, ^, atan2, max, min, abs, eps, nextfloat, prevfloat
- All code guarded by `@static if isdefined(Core, :BFloat16)` (Julia >= 1.11)
- Wires into CI test matrix as 'rules/bfloat16'

Closes #935

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Move BFloat16 rules to MooncakeBFloat16sExt

- src/rules/bfloat16.jl now only contains type-level methods
  (tangent_type, fdata_type, rdata_type, can_produce_zero_rdata_from_type, etc.)
  that work without BFloat16s.jl loaded
- ext/MooncakeBFloat16sExt.jl contains arithmetic infrastructure
  (zero_tangent_internal, _scale_internal, _dot_internal, etc.) and all
  conversion/math rules — using natural BFloat16 arithmetic without
  explicit Float32 intermediates
- test/ext/bfloat16s/ provides the extension test (replaces rules/bfloat16 group)
- Project.toml: BFloat16s added to [weakdeps], [extensions], and [compat]
- CI: rules/bfloat16 matrix entry replaced with {test_type: ext, label: bfloat16s}

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add comment on _dot_internal return type

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add comment on zero_tangent_internal placement

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Guard bfloat16s test for platforms where BFloat16s.BFloat16 !== Core.BFloat16

On aarch64 with LLVM < 19, BFloat16s.jl defines its own primitive type and
Core.BFloat16 has no arithmetic. Tests are skipped there and run on x86_64
(LLVM >= 15) where BFloat16s.BFloat16 === Core.BFloat16.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use #! format: off in bfloat16.jl to avoid indentation inside @static if

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix BFloat16 extension rules and tests

- Fix sin/cos frules/rrules: use separate sin(x)+cos(x) calls instead of
  sincos(x) which infinitely recurses in Julia 1.12 for BFloat16
- Fix sinpi/cospi frules/rrules: use sinpi(x)/cospi(x) directly instead of
  sincospi(x) which returns Float64 and mismatches primal for edge cases
- Add Base.sinpi/cospi overloads for BFloat16 returning correct BFloat16 values
- Add Base.atan(y::P, x::P) overload since two-arg atan is not defined for
  BFloat16 in Julia 1.12
- Add JET and AllocCheck to test deps (required by test_tangent_interface)
- Use FD-friendly test values in [0.25, 0.5) BFloat16 range where finite
  differences are accurate given BFloat16's limited precision
- Add atol=0.5, rtol=0.5 tolerances to test_rule calls to accommodate
  BFloat16 quantization artifacts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Drop sinpi, cospi, and two-arg atan rules and tests

These functions have broken or inconsistent behaviour for BFloat16 inputs
in Julia 1.12 (sinpi/cospi return Float64, atan(::BF16,::BF16) is missing),
so the rules and their test cases are removed rather than worked around.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Tighten BFloat16 test tolerances to atol=0.2, rtol=0.4

The previous atol=0.5, rtol=0.5 were overly loose. Analysis of BFloat16 FD
behaviour guides the new values:

- rtol=0.4 covers the ~24% relative snap error when test values are in the
  [0.125, 0.25) BFloat16 range (spacing 0.000977), where the ε=1e-2
  perturbation rounds to exactly one grid step (0.000977) vs the intended
  ~0.00129, giving ẋ_fd/ẋ ≈ 0.76.

- atol=0.2 covers two edge cases that cannot be fixed by value selection alone:
  · acos(P(0.2)): output ∈ [1,2) with BF16 spacing 0.0078, so ẏ_fd=0 at all
    ε<1e-2; at ε=1e-2 the snap gives |LHS-RHS|=0.159 which needs atol≥0.16.
  · exp10(P(0.249)): chosen so f'(x)≈4.08 just exceeds the 4.0 threshold
    needed for the output perturbation to cross a BF16 boundary, giving a
    non-zero ẏ_fd at ε=1e-2 and |LHS-RHS|≈0.156 < atol=0.2.

All other test values moved to [0.125, 0.25) or [0.2, 0.25) to ensure:
1. Input perturbation is not absorbed (half-spacing 0.000488 < 0.00129)
2. Snap error stays within rtol=0.4
3. rrule tests use normalized tangents (|ẋ|≈1) so ε=1e-2 >> spacing, giving
   accurate FD well within atol=0.2.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add comment explaining BFloat16 test tolerances

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Guard MooncakeBFloat16sExt body with @static if isdefined(Core, :BFloat16)

Core.BFloat16 is only available on Julia >= 1.11. Without the guard the
extension would error on Julia 1.10 LTS when BFloat16s.jl is loaded.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update MooncakeBFloat16sExt.jl

Signed-off-by: Hong Ge <3279477+yebai@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor using statements in MooncakeBFloat16sExt.jl

Removed duplicate using statements for Mooncake and AbstractRNG.

Signed-off-by: Hong Ge <3279477+yebai@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update BFloat16 availability check for Julia version

Signed-off-by: Hong Ge <3279477+yebai@users.noreply.github.com>

* Modify Core.BFloat16 test skipping logic

Updated conditional check for Core.BFloat16 availability based on Julia version.

Signed-off-by: Hong Ge <3279477+yebai@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review comments + fix BFloat16s ext precompilation

- Move can_produce_zero_rdata_from_type into MooncakeBFloat16sExt
- Add nan_tangent_guard to ^ frule/rrule (guards x=0, y<1 → NaN tangents)
- Fix @static if guard to also check BFloat16s.BFloat16 === Core.BFloat16
- Move using/import to module top-level (Julia requires this for precompilation)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Bump version from 0.5.19 to 0.5.20

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix NaN guards in BFloat16 ^ frule and rrule

- frule: guard on z instead of tangent(y) for the log term,
  since z*log(_x) = 0*(-Inf) = NaN when _x == 0 regardless of tangent(y)
- rrule: nest nan_tangent_guard(z, ...) inside nan_tangent_guard(dz, ...)
  to guard the z*log(_x) term against the same 0*(-Inf) = NaN case

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Test NaN guard edge cases for BFloat16 ^ frule and rrule

with x=0, y=0.5, where z*log(x) = 0*(-Inf) = NaN without the guard.
Asserts that neither the forward tangent nor the reverse-mode rdata
contains NaN.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review comments on BFloat16 ^ rules

- Add comments explaining why _x^(_y-1) cannot be rewritten as z/_x
  (0/0=NaN vs the correct Inf at the boundary) and why the nested
  nan_tangent_guard is needed (two independent NaN sources)
- Strengthen NaN guard tests: assert exact values (Inf and zero(P))
  rather than just !isnan; add a second frule call with dx=0 to verify
  the y-term guard independently

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix imprecise comments in BFloat16 ^ rules and tests

- Broaden the _x^(_y-1) note: the "= Inf" claim only holds for 0<y<1;
  rewrite as "gives the correct value at the boundary"
- Test: replace "x-term dominates" with "y-term guarded to 0; x-term = Inf"
- Test: "may be Inf" -> "is Inf" with the exact expression for these inputs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix: rewrite /etc/hosts via inode overwrite to bypass bind mount rename restriction

Use 'cat > /etc/hosts' instead of 'sed -i' to patch BuildKit container
hosts file. sed -i fails with 'Resource busy' on Docker bind mounts because
it tries to rename a temp file over the original. Writing via cat preserves
the inode and avoids the restriction.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: tighten _is_windows_lock_error to match only genuine lock errors

Remove overly broad patterns ('directory not empty', 'device or resource
busy') that could match errors unrelated to Windows file locking.  Add
Windows error code patterns ([WinError 32], [WinError 33]) and the
WinError 33 message text for precise lock detection.

Restructure _handle_worktree_removal_error to always attempt force
removal regardless of error classification, so transient failures like
'directory not empty' caused by a locked file inside still get retried
without being misclassified as a lock error.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(hooks): use precise test exclusion in coverage check

Replace overly broad 'test not in f' filter with precise exclusions
that correctly handle source files containing 'test' in their name
(e.g. beta_tester.py). The new filter checks for /tests/ directories
and test_ file prefixes, which works with both the current flat layout
and the planned subdirectory restructuring.

Verified all hook glob patterns already use recursive-compatible
matching (startswith 'src/pokepoke/', '^src/pokepoke/') so they
will work correctly when files are moved to domain subpackages.

Closes: PokePoke-41tl

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: replace magic number dialog_type=20 with webview.FOLDER_DIALOG

In browse_for_project(), the dialog_type parameter was hardcoded as 20
with a comment referencing webview.FOLDER_DIALOG. This replaces the
magic number with the actual constant for readability and to avoid
breakage if the value changes in a future pywebview version.

Resolves: PokePoke-5b21

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: handle turn.aborted in ProviderRuntimeIngestion to unblock Copilot stop (#16)

* fix: handle turn.aborted in ProviderRuntimeIngestion to unblock Copilot stop

When the user clicks stop during a Copilot turn, CopilotAdapter emits a
turn.aborted runtime event. ProviderRuntimeIngestion only handled
turn.completed for lifecycle transitions, leaving the session stuck in
"running" with a stale activeTurnId — blocking new sends.

Add turn.aborted to the lifecycle event handling so it:
- Transitions session status to "interrupted" (maps to "ready" in UI)
- Clears activeTurnId to null
- Finalizes buffered assistant messages and proposed plans

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* style: fix formatting in ProviderRuntimeIngestion test

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: gate turn.aborted finalization on active turn lifecycle guard

A late or duplicate turn.aborted for a stale turn could incorrectly
transition the session to 'interrupted' and flush buffered assistant
messages from the wrong turn. Gate the finalization block with
shouldApplyThreadLifecycle (which verifies the event's turnId matches
the active turn). Add regression test for stale-turn abort rejection.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

'as unknown' cast to the arrow function return to satisfy
@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-assignment, no-unsafe-member-access,
and no-unsafe-return rules.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: update CI workflow and format terraform code

- TFLint: use --minimum-failure-severity=error (warnings don't fail CI)
- Run terraform fmt -recursive for canonical formatting

fix: resolve ESLint type safety errors in index.test.ts

Add type assertion to JSON.parse result and use bracket notation
for property access to fix @typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-assignment
and @typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-member-access errors.

commit.message:
fix: add type casts to JSON.parse calls in errorHandler.test.ts to fix ESLint type safety errors

Add 'as Record<string, unknown>' casts to all JSON.parse() calls and
'as unknown' cast to the arrow function return to resolve 22 unsafe-any
ESLint errors without changing test logic.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve 36 ESLint type-safety errors in cli.test.ts

Change consoleSpy type from ReturnType<typeof vi.spyOn> to MockInstance
(imported from vitest) to fix unsafe-call, unsafe-member-access, and
unsafe-return errors on .mockRestore() and .mock.calls access.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add ShopScene overlay for selling items and buying seeds

Implements the shop overlay scene with:
- Sell tab: lists sellable inventory items with per-item sell buttons
  and a bulk 'Sell All Materials' action
- Seeds tab: lists all seeds from SEED_DATA with buy buttons
- Scrollable content with Phaser mask + pointer drag
- Panel follows Rule 10 template (backdrop, gold border, header, close)
- All tap targets min 44x44px (Rule 5)
- Typography scale per Rule 9

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve ESLint type safety errors in generatePoliticalLandscape.test.ts

Add type casts for unsafe member access on unknown types:
- Cast data.groups to Array<Record<string, unknown>> for loop access
- Cast data.powerDynamics to Record<string, unknown> for nested props
- Cast numeric values (memberCount, seatShare) as number for comparisons

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
